title: Critical profile
principles: Critical chance determines the critical tier probabilities of an eligible damage instance, while critical multiplier determines the damage reward produced by those tiers.
Critical chance and critical multiplier must be evaluated together because neither statistic alone defines the complete critical profile.
Critical recurrence per eligible instance and critical reward are distinct properties and must not be treated as interchangeable.
Expected critical factor summarizes average long-run contribution per eligible instance but does not describe opportunity frequency, total output, or short-sequence consistency.
Qualitative profile classifications require an explicit comparison context and must not rely on universal critical thresholds.
Critical profiles must remain separate for attack modes, damage components, or conditional states with different supplied values.

aligned: Critical tier probability and critical reward are both favorable within the supplied comparison context.
The two properties reinforce one another at the eligible-instance level.
frequency_weighted: Critical tier probability is more favorable than critical reward within the comparison context.
Critical events occur comparatively reliably, but each tier provides more limited additional reward.
reward_weighted: Critical reward is more favorable than critical tier probability within the comparison context.
Critical events can be valuable when they occur but remain less consistent across short sequences.
limited: Neither critical tier probability nor critical reward is favorable within the supplied comparison context.
The critical relationship provides limited evidence of a defining statistical strength.
undetermined: Critical chance, critical multiplier, or the required comparison context is absent or incompatible.
The critical relationship cannot be classified confidently.

critical_chance_at_or_above_100_percent: Every complete 100 percent of critical chance guarantees one additional critical tier for an eligible damage instance.
The remaining percentage determines the probability of reaching the next tier.
Critical multiplier remains relevant because each higher tier increases the resulting critical damage.
conditional_critical_profile: Preserve the activation condition and evaluate the conditional critical values separately from the base profile.
Do not describe conditional critical chance or multiplier as permanently active.
multiple_critical_profiles: Preserve separate profiles for direct, radial, chained, secondary, alternate-mode, or conditional components when their supplied statistics differ.

exceptions: Do not infer the number or temporal frequency of critical opportunities from critical chance or critical multiplier.
Do not assume that separate attack modes, damage components, or conditional states share the same critical statistics.
Do not infer critical bonuses, guarantees, or tier behavior from the weapon name or unsupported descriptive language.
Do not use expected critical factor as evidence of short-term consistency or complete weapon output.
